summary: Align sources/manifest.toml with what actually landed in corpus/
description:
Reconcile sources/manifest.toml + chunking/ + corpus/ so the manifest reflects what actually landed. Three mismatches found by diffing manifest [[source]] ids against corpus/{tradition}/{text_id}/metadata.toml (chunk_count) and the raw/ + chunking/ inputs.

Reproduce: load manifest, list corpus text dirs with chunk_count, and for each tradition check (a) manifest sources with no corpus dir, (b) corpus dirs with no manifest source, (c) landed dirs with anomalously low chunk_count vs raw byte size.

== Finding A — `mandaean`: listed, never landed (0 chunks). ROOT CAUSE = download dispatch gap ==
Manifest has 2 sources (gnostic-john-baptizer-1, gnostic-john-baptizer-2; format=html_multi; gnosis.org). Reality: no corpus/mandaean/, no raw/mandaean/, no chunking/mandaean/*.toml. The cause is structural, not merely "acquire was never run":
- acquire.py FORMAT_DISPATCH maps html_multi -> the sacred_texts downloader UNCONDITIONALLY (dispatch is by format, not domain). There is no multi-page path for gnosis.org.
- scripts/downloaders/gnosis_org.py exists but is NOT in FORMAT_DISPATCH (dead code) and is single-page only (returns one (text, meta) tuple, no index crawl). Note gospel-of-thomas/philip (gnosis.org, format=html) actually run through generic_html, so gnosis_org.py has never executed.
- Even when routed to sacred_texts, download() only takes the multi-page branch when "index" is in the URL (sacred_texts.py:236). The mandaean URLs are leaf chapter pages (gjb-2-1.htm / gjb-2-2.htm, no "index"), so it would treat them as single pages and run sacred-texts.com layout extraction against gnosis.org markup -> wrong/empty content.
- mandaean is the ONLY html_multi source not on sacred-texts.com, which is precisely why it is the only html_multi source that failed to land; every sacred-texts html_multi source worked.
- Manifest modeling bug too: two leaf chapter URLs are each tagged html_multi as if each were an index. Mead's "Gnostic John the Baptizer" is a multi-section work with its own index/next structure that was never mapped.
Resolve (decision needed): EITHER (1) wire gnosis.org multi-page support — add a gnosis multi-page crawler OR make FORMAT_DISPATCH/downloaders domain-aware, AND repoint the manifest at the real John-Book index, then run acquire+chunk+embed (high cohesion: "only living Gnostic religion", adjacent to Thomas/Philip) — OR (2) remove the 2 manifest entries. NB option 1 is a code change to acquire.py + downloaders, not just config.

== Finding B — `sefer-yetirah`: orphaned duplicate (recommend delete) ==
corpus/jewish_mysticism/sefer-yetirah/ (6 chunks) + chunking/jewish_mysticism/sefer-yetirah.toml exist with NO manifest source. It is the abandoned Sefaria source — its config's translator is "Sefaria Community Translation". The manifest comment (the jewish_mysticism block) explicitly said to REMOVE the sefaria sefer-yetirah entry in favor of sefer-yetzirah (Westcott, sacred-texts jud/yetzirah.htm, 7 chunks, present and correct). The manifest line was removed but the leftover chunking config + corpus dir were never cleaned up.
Resolve: delete chunking/jewish_mysticism/sefer-yetirah.toml + corpus/jewish_mysticism/sefer-yetirah/ (and any raw/ leftover). Then rebuild embeddings/graph so the dropped chunks leave chunk_embeddings + guru.db.

== Finding C — 3 Plotinus singles: broken + redundant (1 chunk each) ==
neoplatonism/plotinus-on-the-beautiful, -on-intelligible-beauty, -on-the-good-or-the-one each landed only 1 trivial chunk. Cause: their format=html URLs point at single Ennead *section* sub-pages, so raw extraction captured 2130 B / 2391 B / 80 B respectively (the 80 B one is essentially empty), and the chunk body still carries nav boilerplate ("Sacred Texts Classics Index Previous Next Section 8..."). The full Enneads are already in plotinus-select-works-index (html_multi, 1432 raw pages, 838 chunks).
Resolve (decision needed): EITHER (1) remove the 3 manifest entries + chunking configs + corpus dirs (cleanest — redundant with the index) — OR (2) re-point them at full-tractate sources if dedicated per-Ennead citation labels are wanted over the index's section labels. If removed, rebuild embeddings/graph.

== Acceptance ==
- manifest [[source]] set ⇄ corpus text dirs are 1:1 (modulo documented html_multi index→family expansions); no orphan corpus dirs; no near-empty (1-chunk, <500 B raw) landed sources.
- Re-running the reconciliation diff reports zero mismatches.
- Any deleted/added chunks are followed by embed_corpus.py + graph rebuild so guru.db (chunk_embeddings, EXPRESSES edges) and export.py output stay consistent — data via pipeline, never hand-edited.

Adjacent (not in scope): README.md "Starter Corpus (v1) — 22 texts / sources 404 pending" and manifest header "v1 — 22 source texts" are stale (now 79 sources, 16 traditions). Flag only.

resolution.note: All 3 children done: abae42da (sefer-yetirah orphan removed), 73c83a22 (gnosis dispatch fixed, Mandaean John-Book landed, 66 chunks), 901712e4 (3 broken Plotinus singles removed). sources/manifest.toml now reconciled with corpus/. Adjacent items spun out as separate tickets (13f72b3d graph_bootstrap; 95e3234c junk-text cleanup). Downstream: guru-web PG re-export pending.
